title: Quiz: Quantum Sampling Applications
description: Test your understanding of quantum sampling applications in finance, physics, and machine learning

questions.question: In quantum option pricing, what speedup does quantum Monte Carlo provide over classical Monte Carlo?
questions.explanation: Quantum Monte Carlo provides a quadratic speedup: achieving precision epsilon requires O(1/eps) quantum queries vs O(1/eps^2) classical samples. This comes from amplitude estimation.

questions.question: What is Value at Risk (VaR) in the context of risk analysis?

options.text: The expected return of a portfolio
The loss threshold exceeded with probability (1-alpha)

questions.explanation: VaR_alpha is the loss threshold such that losses exceed this value with probability (1-alpha). For example, 95% VaR is the loss that is exceeded only 5% of the time.

questions.question: What physical quantity does the partition function Z encode?

options.text: The normalization of the Gibbs distribution and connection to free energy

questions.explanation: The partition function Z = Tr(exp(-beta*H)) normalizes the Gibbs distribution. The free energy is F = -T*ln(Z), and Z encodes all thermodynamic information about the system.

questions.question: In a Quantum Boltzmann Machine, what distribution do samples follow?
questions.explanation: Quantum Boltzmann Machines sample from the Boltzmann distribution p(config) proportional to exp(-beta*E(config)), where E is the energy function defined by the weights and biases.

questions.question: Quantum Monte Carlo can price any type of financial derivative with quadratic speedup, regardless of the payoff structure.
questions.explanation: True. As long as the payoff function can be efficiently encoded into a quantum circuit, quantum Monte Carlo with amplitude estimation provides quadratic speedup. This applies to path-dependent options, exotic derivatives, etc.

questions.question: What is the main challenge in applying quantum sampling to near-term devices?

options.text: Quantum sampling algorithms don't exist yet
The required circuit depths exceed coherence times

questions.explanation: The main challenge is that amplitude estimation requires O(1/eps) Grover iterations, leading to deep circuits that exceed the coherence times of current NISQ devices. Error correction would be needed for practical advantage.

questions.question: How is the ground state energy estimated using quantum thermal sampling?

options.text: By measuring the highest energy state
By taking the limit of thermal energy as temperature approaches zero
By counting the number of eigenstates
By computing the partition function derivative

questions.explanation: As temperature approaches zero (beta -> infinity), the thermal expectation value <H> approaches the ground state energy E_0 because the Boltzmann distribution concentrates on the lowest energy state.

questions.question: Which of the following are valid applications of quantum sampling algorithms? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Monte Carlo integration for high-dimensional integrals
Computing thermal properties of quantum materials

questions.explanation: Quantum sampling is useful for Monte Carlo integration (quadratic speedup), training generative models (sampling from quantum distributions), and computing thermal properties. Sorting is not a sampling problem.
